details: H1: Power<>.exec(uint32_t) is invoked but its parameter i is ignored — power_tile(D0, exponent) called via member-exec. The member-exec path goes through hoisted_init_for_each at boot since this chain has no FPU-clash. Need to check if dispatch correctly routes.

FAILURE AXIS ISOLATED: fp32_dest_acc_en=True fails 50/50 (66/66 = exactly the fp32 dimension). bfloat16 input/output CBs vs Float32 intermediates means format reconfig matters. T1.32 / T1.33 use CopyTileReconfig::None (default) so the chain does NOT emit srca reconfig — copy_tile_init calls only the short-init path that skips reconfig.

H1: T1.32 PackTile<cb_tmp1, Dst::D0, PerTileReserveAndPush> uses default PackTileReconfig::None — does NOT emit pack_reconfig_data_format. Pre-migration code used pack_tile_with_dt(dst0, cb_tmp1) which under FP32_DEST_ACC_EN does pack_reconfig. Previous chain leaves pack format = cb_exp_avg_sq_out (bfloat16) but T1.32 packs to cb_tmp1 (Float32). FIX: add PackTileReconfig::Output to T1.32 PackTile. Also try T1.33 for symmetry.
falsifier: if fp32_dest_acc_en=True case still fails after the pack reconfig fix, hypothesis is wrong

details: H2 (HELPER BUG): eltwise_chain non-clash path NEVER emits PackTile pack_reconfig because hoisted_init_for_each skips pack elements and compute_phase_for_each<false,false> skips transitions. PackTileReconfig::Output is set on T1.32/T1.33 but the helper does not honor it. WORKAROUND test: emit pack_reconfig_data_format(cb_tmp1) before the chain.
falsifier: if explicit pack_reconfig before the chain makes the failing test pass, helper is buggy

details: explicit pack_reconfig_data_format before T1.32/T1.33 chains did not fix failure. Pack format reconfig is NOT the bug.

H3: replace T1.32/T1.33 migrated chain calls with raw LLK (the pre-migration code). If this fixes the test, the eltwise_chain helper is buggy for the Power<>{step} member-exec pattern. If still fails, bug is upstream.
falsifier: if reverting T1.32/T1.33 to raw LLK passes, migration is the bug

details: H3 refuted — reverting T1.32/T1.33 (Power<> chains) to pre-migration raw LLK STILL fails. The Power<> migration is INNOCENT. The bug is elsewhere in the moreh_adam kernel, NOT in commit 1711213980e.

H3+ extended: ALSO reverting T1.34 (moreh_copy_chain<tmp_cb_max_exp_avg_sq, cb_max_exp_avg_sq_out>, AMSGRAD-gated) to raw LLK PASSES the failing test. T1.34 is the buggy site. T1.32/T1.33 reverts were red herrings.

H3++ extended: reverting moreh_copy_chain (CopyTile<Input> + PackTile<Output>) at L203/L263/T1.34 to raw LLK fixes BOTH amsgrad=True AND amsgrad=False cases. moreh_copy_chain helper is the actual bug. T1.32/T1.33 Power<> chains were red herrings.

H2 CONFIRMED at the helper level. Adding WITH_FP32_DEST_ACC(pack_reconfig_data_format(CbOut)) inside moreh_copy_chain (before the eltwise_chain call) makes the failing fp32_dest_acc_en=True test PASS. Root cause: chain helper does not emit pack_reconfig for non-clash chains. T1.32/T1.33 also need pack_reconfig (T1.32 fix-by-explicit-reconfig had been added too).

Proper fix: remove the !is_pack_tile_op_v<ElemT> guard in hoisted_init_for_each. This will cause pack-side transitions (pack_reconfig_data_format) to be emitted at boot for non-clash chains when PackTile<...Reconfig=Output> is used. PackTile::init() is empty so no side-effects.

Reg C ROOT CAUSE: eltwise_chain.inl hoisted_init_for_each at line 1031-1045 skipped PackTile elements via if constexpr (!is_pack_tile_op_v<ElemT>). This meant pack_reconfig_data_format declared by PackTile<...PackTileReconfig::Output> was NEVER emitted for non-clash chains. The implementer was completely wrong about T1.32/T1.33 Power<> being the suspect commit — those were red herrings.
fix: helper-level: remove the !is_pack_tile_op_v guard. Kernel-level: ensure T1.32/T1.33 PackTile annotations include PackTileReconfig::Output and CopyTileReconfig::Input.
result: moreh_adam: 132/132 pass (was 66F/66P). No regressions in helper baseline (453P), softmax (24F unchanged), nll_loss (19F unchanged), layer_norm (48P), adamw (19P), bcast_to (24P), sgd (164P), mean (76P), dot (21P).
